summary: My father's family came from Kansas, specifically Leavenworth. He was a young boy when he left Kansas and moved to San Francisco to work. Although my father's and mother's families didn't know each other back then, both had roots in Kansas before settling in California.

I was born in Oakland in November 1894, right at home on Center Street. My father worked in Oakland making manhole covers and running his foundry business at night after working for Steiger and Kerr during the day. Our family lived in various places around Oakland, including Fifth and Center Street and later Fifth and Grove, where we took care of my grandmother and lived together as a family.

My mother's father brought his children out to San Francisco after my mother's grandmother died. They lived there, and he helped run a stable with cousins that had a hearse for funerals and weddings. My father also lived in San Francisco when he was young and worked for Steiger and Kerr before starting his own foundry business. My parents met in San Francisco, where my mother would visit my father who was an engineer working on engines.
cues: father worked for Steiger and Kerr

summary: My father worked for Steiger and Kerr in San Francisco, the company that made iron stoves. While working there during the day, he became interested in iron and started his own foundry business at night. This experience with Steiger and Kerr helped him learn the trade and eventually launch Phoenix Iron Works.

My father started Phoenix Iron Works in 1901, naming it Phoenix though I don't know exactly why. It began in an old building at Second and Castro in San Francisco, where he poured and cast iron at night after working for Steiger and Kerr. The foundry made manhole covers for Oakland and was a going concern until it moved to a new location a few years ago after the city bought the old property.
